output.summary: Fixed Gutenberg editor z-index conflict on tall TextCards by replacing the ::after pseudo-element used for the split background bottom half with an explicit <span> element. This avoids conflict with WordPress's editor selection styles that override ::after on block wrappers.

filesModified.change: Removed md:after:* classes from useBlockProps className and added conditional <span> element for bottom-half split background on tall cards
filesModified.reason: Gutenberg applies ::after styles with z-index: 1 to block wrappers for selection outlines, overriding the block's decorative ::after (z-index: -1). Using an inner <span> element avoids the pseudo-element conflict.

classNameRemoved.removed: md:after:absolute md:after:inset-x-0 md:after:top-auto md:after:bottom-0 md:after:h-1/2 md:after:bg-accent md:after:rounded-3xl md:after:-z-1
classNameRemoved.reason: These classes created the bottom half of the split background via ::after pseudo-element, which conflicted with Gutenberg's editor selection ::after styles

classNameKept.reason: The ::before pseudo-element for the top half is not affected by Gutenberg's selection styles (Gutenberg only uses ::after for selection outlines)

elementAdded.element: <span className="hidden md:block absolute inset-x-0 bottom-0 h-1/2 bg-accent rounded-3xl -z-1" />
elementAdded.purpose: Provides the same bottom-half split background as the removed ::after classes, but as an explicit element that is not affected by Gutenberg's block selection styles
elementAdded.positioning: First child inside the block wrapper, before the RichText elements

before.nonTallCards: Single ::before pseudo-element covers full card with bg-accent (works correctly)
before.tallCards: ::before for top half + ::after for bottom half (::after conflicts with Gutenberg selection, appears on top of content when selected)

after.nonTallCards: Single ::before pseudo-element covers full card with bg-accent (unchanged, works correctly)
after.tallCards: ::before for top half + explicit <span> for bottom half (no Gutenberg conflict, both backgrounds render behind content correctly)

visualEquivalence.visualDifference: None in the rendered output. The <span> element produces identical styling to the ::after pseudo-element (hidden on mobile, visible on md: breakpoint, absolute positioning covering bottom half with bg-accent and -z-1).

warnings: Webpack performance warnings (asset size limits) - pre-existing, not related to changes
buildStatus.details: Build completed successfully with 'webpack compiled with 3 warnings'. All warnings are pre-existing performance recommendations. TypeScript compilation passed without errors.

syncStatus.details: PHP template (TextCard.php) intentionally left unchanged. The ::after pseudo-element conflict is editor-only because Gutenberg's block selection CSS is only loaded in the editor. The frontend renders without these styles, so the ::after approach works correctly in PHP.

rootCause.issue: Gutenberg editor applies ::after styling to block wrapper elements for selection outlines/highlights
rootCause.appliedStyles: position: absolute, inset: 0, z-index: 1, content: '', outline styles
rootCause.conflict: The TextCard's isTall variant used the same ::after pseudo-element on the same block wrapper div for the bottom half of a split background decoration with z-index: -1. When the block is selected, Gutenberg's higher-specificity selector overrides the block's ::after styles, setting z-index to 1 and stretching it to cover the full card, causing it to render as a visible overlay on top of content.
rootCause.whyBeforeNotAffected: Gutenberg only uses ::after for selection outlines, not ::before. The top-half background using ::before pseudo-element is unaffected.

solution.approach: Replace the ::after pseudo-element with an explicit inner <span> element
solution.reasoning: Gutenberg's selection styles target the ::after pseudo-element specifically on the block wrapper (the element receiving useBlockProps). Using an inner element avoids this conflict entirely because Gutenberg does not apply selection styles to child elements.
solution.implementation: Removed md:after:* classes from the useBlockProps className and added a conditional <span> element inside the block wrapper with equivalent styling: 'hidden md:block absolute inset-x-0 bottom-0 h-1/2 bg-accent rounded-3xl -z-1'
solution.phpUnaffected: The PHP template uses ::after successfully because the frontend does not load Gutenberg's editor styles. No PHP changes needed.

editorTesting: Open Gutenberg editor and add a TextCard with isTall enabled
Verify the split background (top and bottom halves) renders behind the content, not on top
Verify the background maintains the accent color and rounded corners
Toggle isTall off and verify the single background still works correctly
frontendTesting: View the test page at https://ign.localhost/test-masonry-cards/
Verify tall text cards have the split background effect (top and bottom halves)
Verify non-tall cards have a single full background
Verify all backgrounds are accent-colored and behind the text content
responsiveTesting: Test at mobile, tablet, and desktop breakpoints
Verify split background only appears on desktop (md: breakpoint)
Verify single background appears on mobile and tablet for tall cards
